Base directory for this skill: /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/skills/add

# Add Board Item

Create a new item in the `board/` project management system. The system spec is bundled with the plugin at `/Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/spec/board-system-spec.md`.

## Existing Board Items

Pre-loaded board data for duplicate detection:

filepath	type	status	priority	blocked_by	parent	title
./board/tasks/TASK-0003_nasa-data-insignia.md	task	todo	-	-	-	Apply for NASA Data Insignia
./board/tasks/TASK-0011_add-r2-storage-layer.md	task	todo	-	-	-	Add R2 storage layer for APOD image archiving
./board/tasks/TASK-0015_add-thumb-route.md	task	backlog	2	-	NOTE-0018	Add /thumb route to Worker
./board/tasks/TASK-0016_add-dimensions-to-info.md	task	backlog	2	-	NOTE-0019	Add image dimensions to /info response
./board/tasks/TASK-0017_landing-thumbnail-placeholder.md	task	backlog	2	TASK-0015, TASK-0016	NOTE-0018	Add thumbnail placeholder and pre-sized container to landing page
./board/planning/NOTE-0005_add-individual-metadata-routes.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Add individual metadata routes
./board/planning/NOTE-0006_add-why-is-this-needed-section.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Add a "Why Is This Needed" Section That Explains the APOD API
./board/planning/NOTE-0008_generate-daily-og-image.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Generate a daily OG image
./board/planning/NOTE-0010_auto-refresh-landing-page-on-rollover.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Auto-refresh landing page on APOD rollover
./board/planning/NOTE-0014_add-gh-action-auto-deploy-on-merge.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Add GitHub Action to auto-deploy to Cloudflare on PR merge into main
./board/planning/NOTE-0016_local-timezone-apod-rollover.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Local timezone APOD rollover
./board/planning/NOTE-0017_landing-page-critique-apr-2026.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Landing page design critique (April 2026)
./board/planning/NOTE-0018_cf-thumbnail-placeholder.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Generate CF thumbnail placeholder
./board/planning/NOTE-0019_pre-sized-image-container-from-metadata.md	note	inbox	-	-	-	Pre-size image container using metadata

Before creating, check if a similar item already exists. If a close match is found, ask the user if they still want to create a new one.

## Scope Assessment

When the user requests a **TASK**, evaluate whether it is actually EPIC-sized before creating it. Look for these signals:

- Multiple distinct steps or deliverables in the description
- Multiple action verbs joined by "and" (e.g., "add auth and set up roles and integrate SSO")
- Work that spans different code areas or concerns
- Sub-steps that could be completed and shipped independently

**Quick test:** "Could someone meaningfully complete and ship part of this while the rest remains undone?" If yes → likely an EPIC.

If EPIC signals are present, suggest an EPIC + sub-TASKs with a concrete breakdown. For example:

> "This looks like it has multiple independent deliverables. Want me to create an EPIC with separate TASKs for each piece instead?"

**This is a soft suggestion only.** If the user says "just make it a task," comply without pushback.

This assessment only applies to TASK creation — do not second-guess EPICs, NOTEs, or other types.

## Arguments

The user provides the type and title: `/board:board-add task <title>`, `/board:board-add epic <title>`, etc.

If no type is given, ask the user what type of item to create.

## Determine Next ID

Run the ID assignment script:

```bash
bash /Users/grant/Documents/Projects/BoardPM/plugin/scripts/next-id.sh <TYPE> ./board
```

This scans all directories (including `board/archive/` and `.doc-ids`) and returns the next available 4-digit zero-padded ID.

## Create the File

Use the correct directory, frontmatter, and naming convention:

| Type | Directory | Filename Example |
|------|-----------|-----------------|
| task | `board/tasks/` | `TASK-0014_add-batch-processing.md` |
| epic | `board/planning/` | `EPIC-0009_batch-mode.md` |
| adr | `board/planning/` | `ADR-0002_chose-webgpu-over-wasm.md` |
| note | `board/planning/` | `NOTE-0001_explore-wasm-option.md` |
| reference | `board/reference/` | `REFE-0007_webgpu-overview.md` |
| resource | `board/resources/` | `RESO-0001_webgpu-article.md` |

## Frontmatter Templates

Task:
```yaml
---
type: task
status: backlog
priority: 2
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Epic:
```yaml
---
type: epic
status: planning
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

ADR:
```yaml
---
type: adr
status: active
context_date: YYYY-MM-DD
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Note:
```yaml
---
type: note
status: inbox
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Reference:
```yaml
---
type: reference
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

Resource (ask user for URL):
```yaml
---
type: resource
status: inbox
url: <url>
created: YYYY-MM-DD
---
```

## Naming Rules

- Convert title to kebab-case for filename
- Task titles MUST be imperative mood (start with a verb). If the user gives a noun phrase, rewrite it (e.g., "batch processing" -> "Add batch processing support")
- Use today's date for `created`

## After Creation

Show the file path and ask if the user wants to add more detail to the body.


ARGUMENTS: EPICs where appropriate

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-04 06:48:22Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0018_cf-thumbnail-placeholder.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-04-03
5	---
6	
7	# Generate CF thumbnail placeholder
8	
9	Have Cloudflare Image Transformations generate a tiny thumbnail of the APOD image that loads instantly as a placeholder. On hero image load, crossfade from the scaled-up thumbnail to the full resolution.
10	
11	This would solve the empty void users see while the hero image loads on slower connections (flagged as P3 in NOTE-0017).
12	
13	## Approach
14	
15	The `/optimized` endpoint already uses `cf: { image: { ... } }` for Image Transformations (scale-down to 1200px, auto WebP/AVIF). A new `/thumb` route could use the same pattern with aggressive settings:
16	
17	```ts
18	const imageOpts: RequestInitCfPropertiesImage = {
19	  fit: "scale-down",
20	  width: 32,       // tiny thumbnail
21	  quality: 30,
22	  format: "webp",  // always WebP for smallest size
23	  blur: 5,         // optional: pre-blur server-side
24	};
25	```
26	
27	Expected output: ~500B-2KB WebP. Small enough to inline as a base64 data URI in the `/info` response, or to load in parallel with near-zero latency.
28	
29	## Implementation options
30	
31	1. **New `/thumb` route** -- returns the tiny image directly. Landing page fetches it in parallel with the full image. Simple, cacheable independently.
32	2. **Inline in `/info` response** -- add a `thumbnail` field with a base64 data URI. One fewer request, but increases `/info` payload and couples the two.
33	3. **Build-time bake** -- not viable since the image changes daily.
34	
35	Option 1 is cleanest. The landing page would:
36	1. Set the thumbnail as `background-image` on `.hero-image-area`
37	2. Load the full image via `<img>` as today
38	3. On `.loaded`, the image covers the thumbnail naturally
39	
40	## Caching
41	
42	Same two-tier strategy as other image routes (6h primary, 24h stale). Cache key based on ET date like everything else.
43
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-04 06:48:22Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0019_pre-sized-image-container-from-metadata.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-04-03
5	---
6	
7	# Pre-size image container using metadata
8	
9	The landing page already fetches `/info` independently of the image (`fetch(\`${API_BASE}/info\`)`). If the metadata includes image dimensions, the container can be set to the correct aspect ratio before the image loads, preventing layout shift and giving a visible frame for the thumbnail placeholder (NOTE-0018).
10	
11	## Current state
12	
13	- The `/info` response includes `title`, `date`, `explanation`, `url`, `hdurl`, `copyright` -- **no dimensions**.
14	- NASA's APOD API does not return image dimensions.
15	- The hero image uses `max-height: 80vh` with `height: auto`, so the container size is unknown until load.
16	- Current CLS: the image pops in at `opacity: 1` with no reserved space, but since it fades in (no reflow of surrounding content pushed down) the visual shift is minimal. The real problem is the empty void, not layout shift per se.
17	
18	## Approach
19	
20	Add `width` and `height` fields to the `/info` response by measuring the image in the Worker:
21	
22	1. After fetching the APOD image URL from NASA, make a HEAD request or fetch a small portion to read dimensions
23	2. Cloudflare Image Transformations can return original dimensions via the `cf-image` response header when processing -- we could extract them from the `/optimized` fetch
24	3. Cache dimensions alongside the APOD metadata (same 6h/24h two-tier strategy)
25	
26	The landing page would then:
27	```js
28	fetch(`${API_BASE}/info`)
29	  .then(r => r.json())
30	  .then(data => {
31	    if (data.width && data.height) {
32	      const img = document.getElementById('hero-img');
33	      img.width = data.width;
34	      img.height = data.height;
35	      // Browser reserves correct aspect ratio space via width/height attributes
36	    }
37	  });
38	```
39	
40	## Open questions
41	
42	- Is the HEAD request approach reliable for NASA's image CDN, or do we need to fetch actual bytes?
43	- Does `cf: { image: ... }` expose original dimensions in response headers? If so, we get this for free from the existing `/optimized` path.
44	- Should we store the aspect ratio (simpler) or full pixel dimensions (more useful)?
45	
46	## Relationship to NOTE-0018
47	
48	These two notes complement each other: NOTE-0018 provides the visual placeholder, this note provides the correctly-sized container to put it in. Together they eliminate both the empty void and any layout shift.
49
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-04 06:48:23Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0006_add-why-is-this-needed-section.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-03-31
5	---
6	
7	# Add a "Why Is This Needed" Section That Explains the APOD API
8	
9	Add a section to the landing page that explains what the APOD API is, why it exists, and why a proxy like nasapicture.com is useful.
10	
11	## What to cover
12	
13	- **What is APOD?** — NASA publishes a new Astronomy Picture of the Day every day, with a professional explanation written by astronomers. It's been running since 1995.
14	- **The problem** — NASA's API requires an API key, has rate limits (30 req/hr on DEMO_KEY), sometimes returns videos instead of images, and doesn't offer a single stable URL that always points to "today's image."
15	- **What this solves** — nasapicture.com gives you a fixed URL that always returns today's image. No API key, no rate limits (edge-cached), no video days (auto-fallback). One URL, drop it in an `<img>` tag.
16	
17	## Tone & placement
18	
19	- Keep it concise — a few short paragraphs, not a wall of text. Match the existing voice (clever, minimal, precise).
20	- Place it between the hero image and the developer-facing route docs, so non-technical visitors get context before the technical details.
21	- Consider a heading like "Why does this exist?" or "The problem with APOD" — something that invites curiosity.
22
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-04 06:48:24Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0010_auto-refresh-landing-page-on-rollover.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-03-31
5	---
6	
7	# Auto-refresh landing page on APOD rollover
8	
9	If a user leaves the landing page open, the image and caption should update automatically when NASA publishes the new APOD — no manual reload required.
10	
11	## Considerations
12	
13	- NASA publishes around midnight Eastern, but the exact time varies (sometimes delayed)
14	- Polling `/info` periodically and comparing the date to what's currently displayed is the simplest approach
15	- A reasonable interval might be every 15–30 minutes — frequent enough to catch the rollover without hammering the API (which is cached on the Worker side anyway)
16	- When a new date is detected: update the image `src`, caption title, and date, with a fade transition
17	- Should only poll when the tab is visible (`document.visibilityState`) to avoid wasting requests in background tabs
18
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-04 06:48:24Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0016_local-timezone-apod-rollover.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-04-01
5	---
6	
7	# Local timezone APOD rollover
8	
9	Users might find it useful (both in the API and the landing page) to choose to get the new image at midnight in their local timezone, instead of when it actually rolls over in ET.
10	
11	## The problem
12	
13	APOD publishes once per day, keyed to Eastern Time. A user in Tokyo (UTC+9) sees the image roll over at 1pm JST -- awkward if they want "a new picture every morning." Conversely, a user in PST sees it change at 9pm, which might actually be fine.
14	
15	## Possible API surface
16	
17	A query parameter like `?tz=Asia/Tokyo` or `?tz=UTC%2B9` on any image endpoint. The Worker would compute "today" in the requested timezone instead of ET, and request that date's APOD.
18	
19	## Key constraint
20	
21	If a user's local date is ahead of ET (e.g., it's April 2 in Tokyo but still April 1 in ET), the April 2 APOD doesn't exist yet. The Worker would need to fall back to the most recent available date, which is what `findLatestImage` already does via lookback. So this might mostly work out of the box -- the timezone param just changes which date the lookback starts from.
22	
23	## Landing page
24	
25	Could detect timezone via `Intl.DateTimeFormat().resolvedOptions().timeZone` and pass it to the API. Or let users toggle between "NASA time" and "local time" if we want to keep it explicit.
26	
27	## Phasing
28	
29	Nice-to-have, not critical. Phase 1 could just handle timezones behind ET (west of Eastern), which is straightforward -- the requested date always exists. Timezones ahead of ET (where the date might not exist yet) can come later if there's demand.
30	
31	## Related
32	
33	Ties naturally into TASK-0011 (R2 storage layer for image archiving). If we're storing the last N images anyway, serving a timezone-shifted "yesterday" from the archive is trivial. Any tasks spawned from this note are likely blocked by TASK-0011.
34	
35	## Open questions
36	
37	- Caching implications: timezone-varied requests would reduce cache hit rates since each timezone produces a different date key during the rollover window
38
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-04 06:48:25Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/planning/NOTE-0017_landing-page-critique-apr-2026.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: note
3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-04-03T21:30:00-05:00
5	---
6	
7	# Landing page design critique (April 2026)
8	
9	Captured: 2026-04-03 ~9:30 PM CT, dev site at localhost:8788
10	
11	## Anti-Patterns Verdict: Pass
12	
13	No AI slop detected. No purple gradients, glassmorphism, glowing accents, or template layouts. Color palette is disciplined void-black-to-silver monochrome. Typography from Adobe Typekit (Source Serif 4 Display, Source Sans 3, Source Code Pro) is distinctive. Layout trusts the APOD image to carry the page.
14	
15	## Design Health Score: 25/40 (Acceptable)
16	
17	| # | Heuristic | Score | Key Issue |
18	|---|-----------|-------|-----------|
19	| 1 | Visibility of System Status | 2 | Image invisible while loading; silent /info failure |
20	| 2 | Match System / Real World | 4 | -- |
21	| 3 | User Control and Freedom | 3 | Focus mode exits work (Esc, overlay) |
22	| 4 | Consistency and Standards | 3 | Animated-bottom-border reused cleanly |
23	| 5 | Error Prevention | 3 | -- |
24	| 6 | Recognition Rather Than Recall | 2 | Focus toggle unlabeled; copy affordance subtle |
25	| 7 | Flexibility and Efficiency | 1 | No keyboard shortcuts; single path |
26	| 8 | Aesthetic and Minimalist Design | 3 | 10rem dead zone undermines minimalism |
27	| 9 | Error Recovery | 2 | `.catch(() => {})` on /info -- zero feedback |
28	| 10 | Help and Documentation | 2 | Endpoint list is only docs |
29	
30	Cognitive load checklist: 1 failure (low, good).
31	
32	## What's Working
33	
34	1. **Image is the hero** -- fills ~80vh on desktop, everything recedes. Focus mode ("dim the lights") with 1.5s transition is cinematic and on-brand.
35	2. **Usage row nails the pitch** -- "New day, new photo, same URL" + copyable URL + img tag. Entire value prop in two lines. Copy interaction with "Copied" badge provides real feedback.
36	3. **Typography is distinctive** -- Source Serif/Sans/Code Pro family creates hierarchy through subtlety, not volume.
37	
38	## Priority Issues
39	
40	### [P2] Dead zone -- `margin-bottom: 10rem` on `.hero-image-area`
41	Creates massive scroll void on desktop between usage row and endpoints. Users think the page ends. Endpoints section uses `.fade-up` (opacity: 0) and IntersectionObserver may never fire if nobody scrolls through the void.
42	**Fix**: Reduce to 3-4rem.
43	**Command**: `/arrange`
44	
45	### [P2] Focus toggle is hidden
46	Lamp icon (1rem, silver-dimmer) in top-right has no label. `title="Dim the lights"` only shows as browser tooltip. Most users will never discover it.
47	**Fix**: Add text label at desktop widths, or a one-time entrance animation.
48	**Command**: `/delight`
49	
50	### [P2] No accent color in use
51	Design spec calls for amber (#e8a838) but it appears nowhere except the tiny logo dot. All interactive elements (copy buttons, endpoint links, usage URLs) are the same silver-gray as non-interactive text.
52	**Fix**: Apply amber sparingly on copy icon hover, "Copied" badge, endpoint route hover.
53	**Command**: `/colorize`
54	
55	### [P3] Image loading -- empty void on slow connections
56	Hero image starts opacity: 0, transitions on load. On slow connections, users see empty dark rectangle with no loading indication.
57	**Fix**: Add subtle CSS-only loading shimmer or placeholder gradient.
58	**Command**: `/delight`
59	
60	### [P3] Caption layout spreads on wide viewports
61	Title and date in caption span full image width on desktop, feeling scattered rather than intimate.
62	**Fix**: Constrain caption width or cluster elements tighter under the image edge.
63	**Command**: `/arrange`
64	
65	## Persona Red Flags
66	
67	**Jordan (First-Timer)**: Code blocks in usage row look static, not clickable. Copy icon is 12x12px at 50% opacity. Scrolls past usage row, sees void, assumes page is done. Focus toggle: "Is that a settings button?"
68	
69	**Casey (Mobile User)**: Second usage item wraps awkwardly at narrow widths. Copy icon well below 44px touch target. "Copied" badge is tiny and easy to miss.
70	
71	**Pat (Developer Integrator)**: API URL isn't visually distinguished from surrounding text. All four endpoints given equal weight when `/` is 90% use case. `/optimized` endpoint not listed at all.
72	
73	## Open Questions
74	
75	1. **Priority direction**: Issues span layout/spacing (dead zone, caption spread), color/affordance (no accent color, hidden focus toggle), and loading/polish (empty void during image fetch). Which area first?
76	2. **Scope**: Address top 3 only, all issues, or layout only?
77	3. **Color level**: The page is fully monochromatic. Design spec calls for amber (#e8a838). Options: minimal (1-2 touches on copy hover/badge only), strategic (3-4 touches on endpoints, copy, focus toggle), or keep monochrome.
78	
79	## Suggested Action Sequence
80	
81	1. **`/arrange`** -- Fix 10rem dead zone, caption spread, usage row mobile wrapping
82	2. **`/colorize`** -- Introduce amber accent on interactive elements (copy, endpoints, focus toggle)
83	3. **`/delight`** -- Focus toggle discoverability, image loading placeholder
84	4. **`/polish`** -- Final pass after fixes
85	
86	## Minor Observations
87	
88	- `--font-weight-medmium` typo in `_hero.scss:39` -- variable doesn't resolve
89	- Empty `<nav>` element in header -- screen readers announce empty navigation landmark
90	- `/optimized` endpoint missing from endpoint list despite being a valid route
91	- `.usage-row` animation delay 0.8s means primary interactive content invisible for nearly a second
92
```
</details></tool-use>

1	---

3	status: inbox
4	created: 2026-04-01
5	---
6	
7	# Add GitHub Action to auto-deploy to Cloudflare on PR merge into main
8	
9	Set up a GitHub Actions workflow that runs `npm run deploy` (wrangler deploy) automatically when a PR is merged into `main`. This removes the manual deploy step after each merge.
10	
11	## Considerations
12	
13	- Store `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` and `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` as GitHub repository secrets
14	- Trigger on `push` to `main` (which covers PR merges)
15	- Could also run type-check (`npx tsc --noEmit`) as a pre-deploy gate
16
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-04-04 06:48:25Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./board/tasks/TASK-0011_add-r2-storage-layer.md`

```text
1	---
2	type: task
3	status: todo
4	created: 2026-04-01
5	parent: EPIC-0003
6	blocked-by: TASK-0010
7	---
8	
9	# Add R2 storage layer for APOD image archiving
10	
11	Add Cloudflare R2 as a persistent storage/origin layer between edge cache and NASA API. Every image fetched from NASA gets stored in R2, building an automatic archive and providing resilience if NASA is slow or down.
12	
13	## Infrastructure
14	
15	- Create R2 bucket (e.g., `apod-archive`)
16	- Add R2 binding in `wrangler.toml`: `[[r2_buckets]]` with `binding = "APOD_ARCHIVE"`
17	- Update `Env` interface in `src/index.ts` to include the R2 binding
18	
19	## R2 key structure
20	
21	- `{date}/hd.{ext}` — HD image
22	- `{date}/sd.{ext}` — SD image
23	- `{date}/info.json` — metadata snapshot
24	
25	## Worker changes (`src/index.ts`)
26	
27	- Refactor image fetching to layered lookup: Edge cache → R2 → NASA
28	- On R2 miss: fetch from NASA, store in R2 (`bucket.put()`), then serve
29	- On R2 hit: serve directly from R2 with appropriate `Content-Type`
30	- `/optimized` applies `cf.image` transforms on top of R2-served images
31	- Cache metadata in R2 alongside images
32	
33	## Edge caching preserved
34	
35	- `cf.cacheTtl` and `cf.cacheEverything` remain on all responses
36	- R2 replaces NASA as the origin; edge cache sits in front
37	
38	## Cache invalidation
39	
40	Once an image is in R2, it's permanent — if NASA corrects or replaces an image at the same URL, we'd serve the stale version. Low risk (NASA rarely edits published APODs) but worth a safety valve:
41	
42	- For the current day's image, treat R2 as a cache with a short TTL (e.g., re-fetch from NASA if R2 object is <1 hour old and edge cache misses) — allows corrections to propagate same-day
43	- For past dates, serve from R2 unconditionally (immutable after rollover)
44	- Consider an auth-protected manual purge endpoint (e.g., `DELETE /admin/purge/{date}`) to force re-fetch from NASA on demand
45	
46	## Verification
47	
48	- First request stores to R2 (check via `wrangler r2 object get`)
49	- Subsequent requests serve from R2 without hitting NASA
50	- Edge caching still works in front of R2
51	- `/optimized` transforms apply correctly on R2-sourced images
52
```
</details></tool-use>

```md
---
type: epic
status: planning
created: 2026-04-03
---

# Image Optimization Pipeline

Eliminate the empty void users see while the hero image loads, and give the landing page correctly-sized containers before image bytes arrive. Two Worker-side changes (thumbnail route, dimension metadata) feed one landing page integration.

## Goal

A visitor on a slow connection sees: tiny blurred placeholder instantly, correct aspect-ratio container (no layout shift), then full image crossfades in on load.

## Sequence

The tasks have a natural dependency chain:

1. **TASK-0015: Add /thumb route to Worker** -- New endpoint using Cloudflare Image Transformations (32px wide, quality 30, WebP, optional blur). Expected output ~500B-2KB. Same two-tier cache strategy (6h primary, 24h stale) with ET date-based keys.
2. **TASK-0016: Add image dimensions to /info response** -- Extract original width/height during image processing (possibly from `cf-image` response headers on the existing `/optimized` path). Add `width` and `height` fields to the JSON response. Cached alongside existing metadata.
3. **TASK-0017: Add thumbnail placeholder and pre-sized container to landing page** -- Blocked by TASK-0015 and TASK-0016. Fetch `/thumb` and `/info` in parallel on page load. Set container aspect ratio from dimensions, show scaled-up thumbnail as background, crossfade to full image on load.

## Open Questions

- Does Cloudflare Image Transformations expose original dimensions in response headers? If so, TASK-0016 gets dimensions for free from the `/optimized` path.
- Should dimensions be full pixel values or just the aspect ratio?

## Related Notes

- NOTE-0018: Generate CF thumbnail placeholder (design exploration)
- NOTE-0019: Pre-size image container using metadata (design exploration)

```

```md
---
type: epic
status: planning
created: 2026-04-03
---

# Landing Page Polish

Address the design critique findings (NOTE-0017, scored 25/40) and add content and behavior improvements. Work spans layout fixes, color introduction, content additions, and dynamic refresh behavior.

## Areas

### Content
- **NOTE-0006**: Add a "Why Is This Needed" section between the hero and endpoint docs. Explains what APOD is, why NASA's API is awkward, and what nasapicture.com solves. Concise, matching the existing voice.

### Design (from NOTE-0017 critique)
- **[P2] Dead zone**: `margin-bottom: 10rem` on `.hero-image-area` creates a scroll void. Reduce to 3-4rem.
- **[P2] Focus toggle hidden**: Lamp icon has no label, most users never discover it. Add text label or entrance animation.
- **[P2] No accent color**: Amber (#e8a838) specified in design direction but unused. Apply sparingly on interactive elements (copy hover, endpoint links, focus toggle).
- **[P3] Caption spread**: Title and date scatter across wide viewports. Constrain or cluster tighter.
- **Minor**: `--font-weight-medmium` typo, empty `<nav>`, `/optimized` missing from endpoint list, usage row animation delay.

### Behavior
- **NOTE-0010**: Auto-refresh on APOD rollover. Poll `/info` every 15-30min (only when tab visible), compare date, fade-transition to new image/caption on change.
- **NOTE-0016**: Local timezone rollover (nice-to-have). `?tz=` param on API, detect via `Intl.DateTimeFormat` on landing page. Timezones behind ET are straightforward; ahead-of-ET needs fallback (which `findLatestImage` already handles). Ties to EPIC-0003 (R2 archiving makes timezone-shifted "yesterday" trivial).

## Suggested Sequence

1. Layout/spacing fixes (dead zone, caption spread, minor bugs)
2. Introduce amber accent on interactive elements
3. "Why Is This Needed" content section
4. Focus toggle discoverability
5. Auto-refresh behavior
6. Local timezone (deferred until R2 archiving is in place)

## Related Notes

- NOTE-0006: Add a "Why Is This Needed" section
- NOTE-0010: Auto-refresh landing page on APOD rollover
- NOTE-0016: Local timezone APOD rollover
- NOTE-0017: Landing page design critique (April 2026)

```

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```md
---
type: epic
status: planning
created: 2026-04-03
---

# Infrastructure and DevOps

Remove manual steps from the deploy workflow and add durable storage so the service doesn't depend on NASA's uptime for every cache miss.

## Tasks

### TASK-0011: Add R2 storage layer for APOD image archiving

Insert R2 between edge cache and NASA as a persistent origin. Every image fetched from NASA gets stored in R2, building an automatic archive. Layered lookup becomes: Edge cache -> R2 -> NASA.

Key structure: `{date}/hd.{ext}`, `{date}/sd.{ext}`, `{date}/info.json`. Current-day images re-validated against NASA for same-day corrections; past dates served from R2 unconditionally.

R2 archiving also unblocks NOTE-0016 (local timezone rollover), since serving a timezone-shifted "yesterday" from the archive is trivial.

Blocked by TASK-0010.

### NOTE-0014: Add GitHub Action to auto-deploy on merge to main

Workflow triggered on push to `main`. Runs type-check (`npx tsc --noEmit`) as a gate, then `npm run deploy`. Requires `CLOUDFLARE_API_TOKEN` and `CLOUDFLARE_ACCOUNT_ID` as repository secrets.

## Sequence

1. R2 storage layer (TASK-0011) -- larger effort, independent of deploy automation
2. GitHub Action (NOTE-0014) -- small, can be done anytime

## Cross-EPIC Dependencies

- EPIC-0002 NOTE-0016 (local timezone rollover) is blocked by TASK-0011 (R2 archiving)

```
